content: BigQuery is a serverless columnar data warehouse that can scan 1 TB in about 5 seconds. Pricing is $5 per TB scanned (on-demand) or flat-rate slots at $2,000/month per 100 slots. Partitioning by date and clustering by frequently filtered columns can reduce scanned data by 90%+.

BigQuery materialized views auto-refresh and can reduce query costs by up to 99% for repeated analytical queries. They support aggregation functions and inner joins. BigQuery BI Engine provides sub-second query response for dashboards with up to 100 GB in-memory cache.

BigQuery slot-based pricing reserves compute capacity. One slot is approximately one virtual CPU. A 2,000-slot reservation costs $40/hour on-demand or $10,000/month flat-rate. Flex slots allow short-term commitments of 60 seconds minimum for burst workloads.

Cloud Run scales containers from 0 to 1,000 instances automatically with a cold start of 0.5-2 seconds. Pricing is per-request: $0.00002400 per vCPU-second and $0.00000250 per GiB-second. Maximum request timeout is 3,600 seconds (1 hour) with up to 8 vCPUs and 32 GiB memory per instance.

Cloud Run minimum instances setting keeps instances warm to eliminate cold starts at a cost of idle vCPU charges. Cloud Run Jobs execute batch tasks without serving HTTP, supporting up to 10,000 parallel tasks with 24-hour maximum duration.

Vertex AI provides end-to-end ML with AutoML (no-code) and custom training. Training costs range from $1.20/hour (n1-standard-4) to $12.60/hour (a2-highgpu-1g with A100 GPU). Endpoints auto-scale with minimum 1 node and support traffic splitting for A/B testing models.

Vertex AI Feature Store provides a centralized repository for ML features with point-in-time lookups to prevent data leakage. It supports batch serving for training and online serving with <10ms latency for inference. Features are organized in entity types with time-series support.

Google Cloud Pub/Sub delivers messages globally with at-least-once delivery and exactly-once processing via deduplication. Throughput scales to millions of messages per second. Pricing is $40 per TiB of data, with first 10 GiB free monthly. Message retention is configurable from 10 minutes to 31 days.

Azure Cosmos DB supports 5 consistency levels: Strong, Bounded Staleness, Session (default), Consistent Prefix, and Eventual. Strong consistency has 2x the RU cost of eventual. Cosmos DB offers multi-model APIs: SQL (Core), MongoDB, Cassandra, Gremlin (graph), and Table.

Cosmos DB Request Units (RU) measure throughput where 1 RU equals reading a 1 KB item by ID. A point read of 1 KB costs 1 RU; a query scanning 100 items costs 5-50+ RU depending on complexity. Provisioned mode starts at 400 RU/s; serverless caps at 5,000 RU/s per container.

Azure Functions Consumption plan provides 1 million free executions and 400,000 GB-seconds per month. Cold start is 1-3 seconds. Premium plan eliminates cold starts with pre-warmed instances. Durable Functions enable stateful orchestrations with patterns: function chaining, fan-out/fan-in, async HTTP, and human interaction.

Azure Durable Functions orchestrate complex workflows with automatic checkpointing and replay. The fan-out/fan-in pattern launches N parallel activities and aggregates results. Sub-orchestrations enable nesting for up to 1,000 concurrent activity executions per orchestration.

Azure Blob Storage has 3 tiers: Hot ($0.018/GB/month, frequent access), Cool ($0.01/GB/month, 30-day minimum storage), and Archive ($0.00099/GB/month, 180-day minimum, 1-15 hour rehydration). Lifecycle management policies automate tier transitions based on last modified or accessed date.

AKS (Azure) has no control plane charge, EKS (AWS) charges $0.10/hour ($73/month), GKE (Google) charges $0.10/hour for standard mode but offers Autopilot (free control plane, pay per pod). GKE Autopilot manages node pools automatically and enforces best practices by default.

EKS integrates with AWS ALB Ingress and IAM Roles for Service Accounts (IRSA). GKE offers Workload Identity for secure GCP API access and Config Connector for managing GCP resources via Kubernetes YAML. AKS integrates with Azure AD for RBAC and Azure CNI for native VNet networking.

Multi-cloud strategies avoid vendor lock-in by abstracting infrastructure with Terraform or Pulumi, using Kubernetes for container portability, and standardizing on open-source tools (PostgreSQL over Aurora, Kafka over Kinesis). The trade-off is 15-30% higher operational complexity and cost.

Consistent IAM across clouds requires a centralized identity provider (Okta, Azure AD) with SAML/OIDC federation to each cloud. Map roles to least-privilege policies per cloud. HashiCorp Vault provides unified secrets management across AWS, GCP, and Azure with dynamic credentials and automatic rotation.

Reserved Instances save 30-40% for 1-year and 50-60% for 3-year terms across AWS, GCP, and Azure. GCP Committed Use Discounts offer 37% for 1-year and 55% for 3-year. Azure Reserved VM Instances offer up to 72% savings with the flexibility to exchange or cancel.

Spot/Preemptible instances save 60-90% over on-demand: AWS Spot (2-minute interruption warning), GCP Preemptible VMs (24-hour max, 30-second warning), GCP Spot VMs (no max duration), Azure Spot VMs (30-second eviction notice). Best for fault-tolerant batch jobs, CI/CD, and stateless workers.

Cloud cost optimization framework: 1) Right-size instances using utilization metrics (target 60-80% CPU), 2) Use Reserved/Committed for baseline, Spot for burst, 3) Auto-scale to match demand, 4) Delete unused resources (orphaned disks, idle LBs), 5) Use cost allocation tags for accountability.

GCP Cloud Spanner provides globally distributed, strongly consistent relational database with 99.999% SLA. It scales horizontally with no downtime, supporting up to 10 TB per node. Pricing starts at $0.90/hour per node plus $0.30/GB/month for storage.

Azure SQL Database offers serverless tier that auto-pauses after 1 hour of inactivity (configurable) and auto-resumes on first connection with ~1 minute cold start. vCore pricing starts at $0.5145/hour. Hyperscale tier supports databases up to 100 TB with near-instant backups.

GCP Cloud CDN integrates with Cloud Load Balancing and supports cache keys based on headers, cookies, and query parameters. Cache hit ratio typically reaches 85-95% for static content. Cache invalidation propagates globally in under 10 seconds. Pricing is $0.02-0.08/GB for egress.

Azure Front Door combines global load balancing, CDN, and WAF in a single service. It provides <5ms routing decisions, instant failover, and supports header-based routing. Standard tier starts at $0.01/GB for data transfer plus $0.01 per routing rule per hour.

Cloud FinOps practices include: monthly cost reviews with stakeholders, anomaly detection alerts (>20% deviation), showback/chargeback models by team/project, rightsizing automation, and commitment coverage tracking. Mature organizations achieve 20-30% cost reduction in the first year of FinOps adoption.
